title: Â£43 Million UK Aviation Boost: Advancing Hydrogen, SAF, and Cleaner Flights
content: The UK Government has announced a Â£43 million funding package to accelerate green aviation R&D, aiming for net zero by 2050. A key focus is enabling hydrogen propulsion; funds will help the Civil Aviation Authority develop necessary regulations for hydrogen fuel and support zero-emission aircraft technologies. The initiative also bolsters the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) market by funding tracking systems in regions like Africa and the Caribbean to ensure a level playing field. This complements the upcoming SAF Bill, which offers price guarantees for producers, and sits alongside an additional Â£63 million allocated for new SAF production plants. Transport Secretary Heidi Alexander highlighted these technologies as vital for decarbonizing the sector while facilitating expansions at Heathrow, Gatwick, and Luton.
author: Analysts Account
